text:
Every evening during autumn, there's a magical fox that dances around cherry trees. One particular evening, the fox did not leap gracefully. Instead, it twisted its feet awkwardly, tumbling head over heels. Despite the fall, it stood up, licked its paw clean and resumed its dance.

As the stars began twinkling overhead, something extraordinary happened. A group of owls perched nearby noticed the fall. One particular wise owl hooted softly. Then another. Soon, all were hooting. They swooped down, circle-countenance serious.

They flew straight toward the fox.

The fox froze.

One owl hootified softly.

The fox tilted its head.

Two more owls joined.

All hooted gently.

With surprising grace, they enveloped the fox.

They didn't speak.

Just gentle whispers.

A week passed. The owls stayed.

One morning, a young chick peeked out. It saw the owl circle.

The owl tilted its head.

That evening
